title: Citrate Complex of Chromium
author: K. K. Tripathy
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Chromium citrate complex has been studied by  p H titration method. Cr 3+  reacts with the citrate ligand at lower  p H forming a neutral complex C liberating three protons. At higher  p H the complex C dissociates to anionic complexes C 1  -  and C 2-  in two steps. The equilibrium constants of the reechoes  \(Cr_{3+}+H_3Cit \rightleftharpoons C+3H^+, \)   \(C \rightleftharpoons C_1^-+H^+\) and  \(C_1^-\rightleftharpoons C_2^{-2}+H^+\)  are found to be 2.79 X 10 -6 , 6.40 X 10 -6  and 9.16 X 10 7  at 25°C respectively. 

 The   hydrolytic constant for the hydrolysis of Cr 3+  according to the equation  \(Cr_{3+} +H_2O\rightleftharpoons CrOH^{3+} +H^+\)  is found to be 4.58 X 10  -1 .
steps_raw:
Chromium citrate complex has been studied by  p H titration method. Cr 3+  reacts with the citrate ligand at lower  p H forming a neutral complex C liberating three protons. At higher  p H the complex C dissociates to anionic complexes C 1  -  and C 2-  in two steps. The equilibrium constants of the reechoes  \(Cr_{3+}+H_3Cit \rightleftharpoons C+3H^+, \)   \(C \rightleftharpoons C_1^-+H^+\) and  \(C_1^-\rightleftharpoons C_2^{-2}+H^+\)  are found to be 2.79 X 10 -6 , 6.40 X 10 -6  and 9.16 X 10 7  at 25°C respectively. 

 The   hydrolytic constant for the hydrolysis of Cr 3+  according to the equation  \(Cr_{3+} +H_2O\rightleftharpoons CrOH^{3+} +H^+\)  is found to be 4.58 X 10  -1 .
